user_symptom: we experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates with the CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics and GetMetricData APIs when requesting metrics older than 3 hours in the US-EAST-1 Region.
customers experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch metrics for load balancers in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.

some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Metrics PutMetricData and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents APIs in the AP-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state, and some metric data may be missing.

We can confirm increased error rates for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-1 Region. Customers running queries using the StartQuery API may not be able to successfully schedule the query execution. Some customers experienced increased delays for log events in query results in CloudWatch Logs Insights in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We experienced increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

a small number of instances were unavailable due to power loss in a single Availability Zone (usw1-az1) in the US-WEST-1 Region. Some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks for instances that were not impacted by the power event.
we experienced degraded performance for some EBS volumes in the US-EAST-2 Region. During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks.
customers may have experienced elevated alarms API faults and delayed alarms in the EU-WEST-2 Region.

We are investigating increased faults for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Log events and metrics may be delayed. Alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics. Some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log events in the US-WEST-2 Region.

We are investigating elevated rate of API faults and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region. Scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-EAST-1 Region. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully.

some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
customers experienced elevated API fault rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Some metrics were also delayed initially.
some customers may have experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch ELB metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region. APIs also returned insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks and CloudWatch alarms transitioned into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on the delayed metrics.
We experienced increased connection errors and latencies for CloudWatch APIs. Some CloudWatch metrics are delayed.
some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated error rates and delays when running CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-2 Region.
customers experienced elevated errors listing metrics via the CloudWatch ListMetrics API and the CloudWatch Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.

customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Log events and metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch metrics extracted from CloudWatch Logs in the AP-SOUTHEAST-2 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region. Log events and metrics may be delayed. Alarms may transition into "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" state if set on delayed metrics.
customers experienced increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region.

scheduled events are not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-WEST-2 Region. AWS Lambda functions that are invoked in response to CloudWatch events may not be getting invoked successfully. There were delays delivering scheduled CloudWatch events.

some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into the INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

we experienced increased provisioning latencies for Network Load Balancers in the US-EAST-1 Region, and are continuing to experience delays in the propagation of CloudWatch metrics for load balancers.

customers experienced elevated faults when making GetMetricStatistics API requests for metrics data more than two days old in the US-EAST-1 Region and CloudWatch Dashboards were unavailable. CloudWatch logs customers experienced elevated API faults and delayed log events.
we experienced latency in the ability to search contacts, and the inability to add contacts to rooms and calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latency for the Identity, Registry and Rules Engine APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies for invokes in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated latency and API error rates for the Amazon EC2 Container Service APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.
ECS experienced increased API error rates, latencies, and task launch failures. Fargate task launches are currently experiencing increased insufficient capacity errors.
we experienced increased error rates in the GetRecommendations API in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates in AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for the AWS Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.
a subset of Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics applications were not processing data in US-EAST-1 Region.
the VPC Management Console experienced elevated error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
We are investigating increased error rates for ELB APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies for some AWS services within the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs, and elevated launch failures for new EC2 instances in the US-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced elevated failure rate for sample app and custom SDK generation and cloud logic API creation for all Mobile Hub projects.
we experienced elevated API error rate in the US-EAST-2 Region, restricted to control plane operations for API Gateway Version 2 API (WebSocket and HTTP APIs).
we experienced increased error rates affecting the Billing Console including the Dashboard and the Bills page.
we experienced increased API error rates and latencies as well as increased back-end instance registration times in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced an increased error rate while executing deployments, in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates for the ECR APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced delays in running steps, as well as starting and terminating Amazon EMR clusters in the US-EAST-1 Region.
some Amazon Aurora clusters experienced increased database create times and cluster unavailability in the CA-CENTRAL-1 Region.
we experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch Metric Streams APIs in the AF-SOUTH-1 Region. Customers may have experienced issues creating or updating their metric streams.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced increased API fault rates and latencies in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
customers experienced a significant impairment to their Amazon Kinesis Data Streams API operations, including increased error rates, inability to write or read data, and increased latencies.
some Amazon Aurora clusters experienced increased database create times and cluster unavailability in the AP-SOUTHEAST-2 Region.

We have confirmed increased error rates for binding certificates and first time certificate issuance in the US-EAST-1 Region. We have also confirmed increased error rates regarding validation emails in all regions. We also saw delays in validation e-mails in all regions.
we experienced increased error rates affecting a small number of customers running Amazon Athena queries in the US-EAST-1 Region.

customers experienced elevated latency and error rates in response to IAM API requests, as well as delays in describing recently created or modified IAM resources. Propagation of IAM API updates was delayed in the ME-SOUTH-1, EU-SOUTH-1, AP-EAST-1, and AF-SOUTH-1 regions, and newly created or recently updated IAM users, credentials, roles, and policies may not have been available for authentication and authorization in those regions.
S3 customers saw elevated errors and latency for a subset of API requests to create and manage Amazon S3 buckets.
AWS Single Sign-On (SSO) customers using an external identity provider that is enabled in the US-EAST-1 Region experienced issues when attempting SSO authentication for the AWS console and services.
we experienced increased API error rates and connection errors to WorkSpaces in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for API calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.
customers may have experienced some delayed alarms in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased Workmail Outlook latencies in the US-EAST-1 Region impacting the sending and receiving of new messages or appointments in Outlook clients. Between 10:16 AM and 11:07 AM PDT we experienced degraded WorkMail Outlook performances in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates and latencies affecting multiple services and the AWS Management Console in the SA-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced elevated API error rates and connection failures in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 2:50 AM and 4:55 AM PDT we experienced increased API error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased errors when launching new WorkSpaces in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced increased Git Push/Pull error rates via the SSH protocol in the US-EAST-2 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for send and receive operations for FIFO Queues in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for Amazon S3 requests in the US-EAST-2 Region.
we experienced elevated API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for AWS Lambda calls from Amazon Lex bots in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased queue latency for Build operations in the US-EAST-1 Region.
customers may have experienced 500 Errors or "Request Limit Exceeded" when calling an affected API, or using the EC2 Management Console.

We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region. Between 3:46 PM and 5:08 PM PDT we experienced slightly increased latencies for the EC2 APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
we experienced authentication issue affecting some workspaces in the US-WEST-2 Region.
customers may have experienced elevated latency and errors when accessing AWS APIs and consoles in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates in starting clusters in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
we experienced an increased error rate for the SubscribeToShard API, affecting some customers in the US-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced increased error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
Amazon ElastiCache experienced increased latencies while provisioning new ElastiCache nodes and elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced increased API error rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

we experienced timeouts and elevated latencies for a small proportion of AWS Lambda functions in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Impairments were limited to a small proportion of non-VPC Lambda functions that made outbound network connections during this time.
we experienced increased error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Starting at 5:03 PM PDT, we experienced increased ENI attachment times for newly launched EC2 instances and newly attached ENIs within a single Availability Zone (USE1-AZ4) in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Starting at 5:35 AM PDT we experienced power and network connectivity issues for some instances, and degraded performance for some EBS volumes in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances and increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs within the US-EAST-2 Region. Some EC2 instances and EBS volumes experienced a loss of power within a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-2 Region.
customers experienced elevated DNS failures when querying Route 53 Resolver for certain hostnames in a single Availability Zone (use1-az4) in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced degraded performance for a small number of EBS volumes in a Single Availability zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced connectivity issues affecting some database instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region.
we experienced elevated packet loss to and from some destinations on the Internet in the SA-EAST-1 Region.
some instances were unreachable and some EBS volumes experienced degraded performance in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced increased error rates for instance launches in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Starting at 1:18 PM PDT we experienced connectivity issues to some EC2 instances, increased API errors rates, and degraded performance for some EBS volumes within a single Availability Zone in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.

some instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Some EBS volumes experienced degraded performance during this time period and launches of new EC2 instances and the creation of new EBS volumes experienced increased error rates.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to our service. Gateways were unable to perform out of cache reads and appeared offline in the SGW console. Gateways continued to accept writes but were unable to upload them to the AWS service.
we experienced increased error rates for instance launches in a single availability zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experience periods of network connectivity issues for NAT Gateway within the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
some newly launched instances experienced Internet and EC2 Public IP address connectivity issues in the US-GOV-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating connectivity issues affecting some instances in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. Some instances are impaired and some EBS volumes are experiencing degraded performance. Some EC2 APIs are also experiencing increased error rates and latencies.
customers experienced elevated network packet loss that impacted connectivity to a subset of Internet destinations.
we experienced network connectivity issues affecting Direct Connect customers using the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
some database instances in the AP-SOUTH-1 Region are experiencing degraded performance or are unavailable.
a small number of instances experienced connectivity issues in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
some customers experienced connectivity issues from the East Coast of the United States to AWS services.
we experienced elevated error rates for instance related APIs and new instance launches in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating increased error rates for new launches in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We can confirm increased API error rates and connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region. We continue to investigate connectivity issues from some instances to some AWS services in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced meeting availability issues in the US-EAST-1 Region.
some RDS instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.
we experienced an Internet connectivity issue with an external provider outside of our network which affected traffic from some end-user networks and the AP-SOUTH-1 Region.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to our service. Gateways were unable to perform out of cache reads and appeared offline in the SGW console. Gateways continued to accept writes but were unable to upload them to the AWS service.

we experienced increased failures for newly launched instances within the US-EAST-2 Region. Some APIs, such as those used to attach Elastic IP addresses to instances, were also affected by this event. Some AWS services, such as Elastic Load Balancing and Elastic Map Reduce, experienced provisioning delays due to failures in launching new EC2 instances.
We are investigating increased EC2 launch failures and networking connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone (USE1-AZ4) in the US-EAST-1 Region.
customers experienced Internet connectivity issues for some networks to and from the US-EAST-2 Region.
customers experienced increased DNS query timeouts in a single Availability Zone (use1-az6) of the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced intermittent delays in propagating CloudFront distribution changes to some edge locations.

We experienced increased change propagation latency and elevated error rates for CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone calls to the Route 53 API.
customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits.
we experienced delays in propagating changes to CloudFront distributions to our edge locations due to the ACM issue in US-EAST-1.

We are investigating elevated error rates and elevated latency in multiple edge locations. We can confirm elevated error rates and high latency accessing content from multiple Edge Locations, which is also contributing to longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.
We are currently experiencing elevated error rates for the Route 53 Management Console.
we experienced elevated DNS resolution issues for some instances in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
we experienced longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.
We experienced longer than usual reporting delays for Invalidations and CloudFront configurations to edge locations. CloudFront Real-time Metrics were not available.
This is causing DNS lookups through these resolvers for a small number of AWS names to fail.
we experienced delays in propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.

We experienced increased change propagation latency and elevated error rates for CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone calls to the Route 53 API.
we experienced increased delays in validation and issuance for DNS validated domains in the US-EAST-1 Region.
we experienced SSL Handshake and BadRequest errors to some CloudFront edge locations.

customers experienced delays propagating changes submitted to the Route 53 API, as well as increased API error rates from 1:55 PM until 3:20 PM. This also affected provisioning of new resources that rely on Route 53 DNS.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. This did not impact queries to existing DNS records.

domain nameservers may unintentionally change for domains registered under .ch, .com.au, .co.nz, .es, .im, .it, .jp, .net.au, .nl, .nz, .se and .sh, which are registered through our third party registrar partner.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers.
some customers in Japan had a small percentage of DNS lookups fail when resolving CloudFront distributions.
customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.
we experienced increased errors when making changes to some hosted zones, and creating new hosted zones using the Route 53 API and Console.

customers might have experienced delays in reporting status change of CloudFront invalidations. During this time, all CloudFront edge locations were consuming configuration changes and invalidations normally but were not updating status changes in console or via CloudFront APIs.
CloudFront experienced longer than usual propagation times to propagate certain configuration changes to a few of our edge locations.
some Non-S3 CloudFront origins domains hosted by a single external DNS provider experienced intermittent resolution errors.

customers experienced issues resolving newly created public EC2 instance names to private IPs within newly created VPCs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Creation of new Route 53 Resolver Endpoints was also delayed during this time.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits to a single Route 53 edge location. This does not impact queries to existing DNS records.
We are investigating slow propagation of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers. Requests to the ChangeResourceRecordSets API are currently being throttled.
we experienced increased propagation times of DNS edits. Queries to existing DNS records were not affected by this issue.
